programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.sub-title: Judge Explains The Rules To Brooks
programme.desc: A survivor of the deadly Christmas parade crash testified about the moment the SUV hit her and her son. She said that the car ran over her left foot. They were among the sixty-one people injured that day.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: A survivor of the deadly parade crash told jurors how her co-worker was killed when a red SUV slammed into the crowd. This deadly crash left a scar on Waukesha, Wisconsin, a community of 70,000.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.sub-title: From Domestic Disturbance To Violent Road Rage
programme.desc: Immediately after the deadly Christmas parade crash, people wondered was this a case of domestic terrorism and could multiple people be involved?

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Jurors heard the harrowing details of what survivors experienced that day when six people died and 61 were injured. Jurors listened as parents recalled how their children were injured in the deadly Christmas parade crash.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Jurors heard how a traditional family event in their community turned tragic in seconds during the deadly Christmas parade crash. Parents testified about finding their children and rushing them to the hospital.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors heard more from the medical examiner who testified on how Botham Jean was killed.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: The state's case focused a lot on the role and responsibility of an off-duty police officer.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Crime Scene Analyst Breaks It Down
programme.desc: A crime scene analyst took the stand to tell jurors what her team found in Botham's apartment.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: Investigators looked into every part of the building where Botham was killed.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Two different forensic pathologists examined the remains of Rosemarie Essa and at first concluded that her death was undetermined. It took weeks before doctors linked this to a poisoning. Dr. David Dolinak told jurors his conclusion after the autopsy report I didn't know why she died. Next, the defendant's demeanor after he found out his wife had died was brought back up for the jury. Dr. Dolinak recalled what he considered unusual conversations with Yazeed Essa.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The cross examination began on the forensic pathologist who did the autopsy on Rosemarie Essa.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.sub-title: First Responders To The Car Crash
programme.desc: The first set of police officers took the stand. This first one was a records keeper who released Rosemarie Essa's car from police custody to her husband. Jurors learned more about the car accident Rosemarie was involved in before she died. Prosecutors believed the defendant wanted his wife to die by poisoning but have it look like a car crash. These first responders arrived on the scene of this accident only to come across an unresponsive woman in her car.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The firefighter-paramedic on the stand explained to the jury how his team scrambled to try to save the life of Rosemarie Essa. She had been in a minor car accident. But even after she died, doctors were left wondering what exactly took her life. Next, the victim's older brother took the stand. Jurors heard another side of the story about his sister meeting and marrying the defendant, Dr. Yazeed Essa.

programme.desc: A Neo-Nazi turned Islamic jihadist shoots two of his roommates after an argument.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer

programme.title: Interview With a Killer

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: December 1987 in Ohio, the nude body of 17-year-old Barbara Blatnik is found face down on a rural road. She had been violently raped and strangled.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy is used to resolve the 1988 murder of a mother killed in front of her toddler.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This is the story of four innocent victims and how their attacker became another successful target of the Bloodline Detectives.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1981, the body of Sylvia Quayle is discovered by her father in her Colorado home: she had been sexually assaulted, stabbed and shot. After a false confession and decades of investigative work, science breaks this case open.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1983, the Seabrook, Texas community is shaken when 20-year-old waitress Susan Eads is found murdered. Decades later, can modern forensic techniques finally find the perpetrator?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Thirty years after college student Cathy Sposito is brutally killed on a hiking trail in Prescott, Arizona, can a groundbreaking forensic tool finally identify her killer?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1996, Danielle Houchins is found murdered. DNA technology finally unmasks the killer in the 2010s.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: The episode follows the investigation of uncovering a serial killer and how DNA genetic genealogy solved the murders.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a group of political extremists robbed a bank in Washington using homemade bombs and military precision to escape. The FBI learned that the robbers might be preparing for a small-scale war with law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980s, a pair of serial bank robbers took hostages and emptied bank vaults across the Midwest.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In February 1988, a record store clerk in Ohio was fatally shot in his van. After discovering thousands of dollars in the vehicle, police knew that robbery was not the motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Two young mothers vanished from East London's streets. At first unconnected, the cases came together when evidence found in Derek's home showed both women suffered the same violent fate at his hands.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: At 18, Christa Pike commits a brutal murder and becomes one of the youngest ever women sent to death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: Arthur Shawcross, known as the 'Genesee River Killer,' was a serial killer who murdered eleven women.

programme.desc: Police charge a mentally unstable man with his wife's murder, but a medical examiner discovers that she died of natural causes.

programme.desc: In 1996, Indiana police identified Richard Alexander as the "River Park Rapist" who assaulted four women that year. Later, DNA analysis casts doubt on his conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police find evidence that would prove what had happened and who was responsible.

programme.desc: Susie Mowbray was charged for her husband Bill Mowbray's death, but her son was convinced of her innocence.

programme.desc: Exploring how an American hit single from 1966 -- "The Cheater" by Bob Kuban and the In-Men -- foreshadowed the demise of the group's lead singer, Walter Scott.

programme.desc: In 1992, Laura Houghteling disappeared from her Bethesda home and was never seen again. Laura's bedroom was searched and forensic science was used to direct them a to prime suspect.

programme.desc: A child was killed in a hit-and-run. The prosecutor has an accident reconstruction expert come to trial.

programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.desc: Soon after becoming pregnant by Dr. Maynard Muntzing, firefighter Michelle Baker's health quickly deteriorates. Eventually, she raises suspicions that Muntzing is doing her harm.

programme.desc: Look at the Princess finds Crichton, Aeryn, Rygel, D'Argo, and Chiana on a planet about to crown their new monarch. The problem is, the Princess Katralla, cannot ascend unless she finds a mate genetically compatible to her poisoned DNA.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E13 - Look At The Princess, Part 3: The Maltese Crichton
programme.desc: John Crichton faces eighty years as a statue. That is, until the Scarran Cargn decapitates the statue and drops the head into a vat of acid.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E12 - Look At The Princess, Part 2: I Do, I Think
programme.desc: The Queen offers Crichton a choice; marry her daughter the crown princess, or she'll turn him over to Scorpius.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E14 - Beware Of Dog
programme.desc: The crew bring aboard an alien tracker to remove some creatures that came aboard hidden in some cargo.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E15 - Won't Get Fooled Again
programme.desc: Crichton has returned to Earth. Or has he always been on Earth all this time? People from his past and present appear. Is this some trick, if so who is behind it and why.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E17 - The Ugly Truth
programme.desc: Crais and Talyn return. The crew are put on trial for an attack that Talyn made on an alien ship.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E18 - A Clockwork Nebari
programme.desc: A Nebari group come aboard Moya and attempt to mind cleanse everyone using a drug. Pilot and Rygel are the only two who can resist the effects. Crichton also remains unaffected due to Scorpius' neural clone.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E19 - Liars, Guns And Money, Part 1: A Not So Simple Plan
programme.desc: Stark plans to rob a less-than-reputable bank to get them money for a sizable purchase of slaves, but things don't quite go the way he thinks they will.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E20 - Liars, Guns And Money, Part 2: With Friends Like These
programme.desc: With the loot from the Depository heist at hand, D'Argo anxiously waits to make contact with the Slave Traders.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E21 - Liars, Guns And Money, Part 3: Plan B
programme.desc: The crew of the injured Moya must rely on the help of disorganized mercenaries to save Crichton from Scorpius.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S3 E1 - Season Of Death
programme.desc: D'Argo and Stark try to heal John, left with his head open on an operating table, while Scorpius attempts to avoid discovery. Zhaan makes a great personal sacrifice, while Jothee and Chiana commit an indiscretion.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S3 E2 - Suns And Lovers
programme.desc: Moya docks at a commerce station so the crew can spend some of the money they acquired from the depository. Disaster strikes however when the station is hit by a storm.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S3 E3 - Self Inflicted Wounds, Part 1: Could'a, Would'a, Should'a
programme.desc: Crichton learns the elusive technology that could allow him to return home to Earth, but while he is preoccupied with the consequences a crew member unsuspectingly nears death.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S3 E4 - Self Inflicted Wounds, Part 2: Wait For The Wheel
programme.desc: The crew work out a way of getting free from the other ship and out of the wormhole. Things become more complicated when their new 'friends' begin to sabotage Moya and Crichton picks up a transmission from Earth.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.desc: Stark accidentally sends himself, Crichton, Aeryn. D'Argo and Jool back in time where they alter the timeline and are forced to interfere further to restore it.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S3 E7 - Thanks For Sharing
programme.desc: Moya rendezvous with Talyn who is seriously damaged. They hide in the atmosphere of a planet to prevent the Peacekeepers from finding them. D'Argo and Rygel go down to the planet to find some Clorium, a drug that will help Talyn recover.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S3 E8 - Green Eyed Monster
programme.desc: Talyn gets swallowed by a Budong, and it is up to Stark to save them; Crichton, already distrustful of Crais, believes he and Aeryn are having an affair while Talyn himself soon shows his hatred of the human.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.desc: The crew of Moya start blacking out and missing portions of time. Pilot becomes inhabited by another entity who needs to 'taste' each crew member to determine if they too are being inhabited.

programme.title: Farscape
programme.desc: Talyn lands on a planet covered mostly with vegetation that will help him to heal. Crichton, Aeryn and Crais leave the ship in search of Xhalax.
